Mtn For Terminating Sanctions
Off Calendar May-30-2013 Continued To Jun-19-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance. The tentative ruling is adopted. Defendant TRADER JOE'S COMPANY, INC.'S Motion for Terminating Sanctions is continued on the Court's own motion to June 19, 2013. Plaintiff's opposition asserts that all outstanding discovery has been answered, however, the opposition does not contain the responses to Special Interrogatories or verifications to any responses other than the form interrogatories. No later than June 5, 2013, Plaintiff's counsel shall file a supplemental declaration attaching these items and also affirming that all documents that were promised to be produced have in fact been produced. Defendant may file a response to the supplemental declaration no later than June 12, 2013 and address whether terminating sanctions continue to be warranted under the guidelines articulated by the Courts of Appeal (See, e.g., Van Sickle v. Gilbert (2011) 196 Cal. App. 4th 1495 ; Doppes v. Bentley Motors, Inc. (2009) 174 Cal. App. 4th 967) if all outstanding discovery has been answered. Courtesy copies are to be provided to Department 302 on the date of filing. Order signed. Judge Pro Tem: David McDonald; Clerk L. Fong; matter not reported. (302/JPT)
